docs.title: 🐾 Welcome
docs.text: This private site contains all the up-to-date instructions for looking after Giles and Parker.

docs.title: ⭐️ Notes
docs.text:
1. Pick Parker up only when necessary, supporting both his chest and rear. Do not lift him by the chest alone.
  2. Giles can be quite unpredictable. Always watch your step and check before sitting down. I do not recommend picking him up. It is better to guide him with your voice and gestures while giving him space. Hold him securely and support both his chest and rear if you absolutely need to pick him up.
  3. Giles is hard of hearing. Always show him your hands before touching him, and announce your presence by tapping gently in front of him if you need to wake him up. Always touch him from under the chin and chest, never over his head, neck, or back.
  4. Keep the bedroom door closed at all times.
  5. A foldable mattress with a fitted sheet is set up by the couch. Feel free to use it or sleep in the bedroom, whichever you prefer.
  6. To turn on/off lights in the living room or bedroom: "Alexa, living room/bedroom on/off," or "Alexa, lights on/off."
  7. Guide Parker to use the jumping cushion or ramp when getting on or off the couch.
  8. Parker does not use the ramp outside.
  9. Please do not walk the dogs. Indoor and yard playtime is sufficient.
  10. Please remove your shoes at the door. Disposable indoor slippers are provided.
  11. Please do not bring any guests.
  12. The dogs like to snuggle and sleep on blankets. Please keep the couch blankets spread out.
  13. You don’t need to pick up poop in the yard. However, if you do, please discard the waste bags in the Pet Genie bin by the porch.
  14. On rare occasions, the dogs may have poop hanging due to hair or soft stool, causing them to crouch-walk. Please use the provided gloves, poop bags, and wet wipes to help clean them if needed.

docs.title: Morning/Wake-up (7 AM - 8 AM)
docs.text:
• Potty Break

    • Check if the gate is closed and locked.
    • Watch out for people and animals near the fence or passing by, since Parker likes to bark at them. You might want to let them pass before letting Parker out unless he really needs to go potty.
    • I let them stay outside to sniff and explore as long as they like, unless they are barking.
    • Feel free to stay inside by the door, but watch them at all times.
    • They will come to the door when they want to come inside. Sometimes, Giles might get scared and linger by the door even when he needs to go. Make sure to give him enough time and space, and he should go down the ramp to potty.
      • Giles usually tends to poop in the morning after breakfast, but sometimes right after waking up.
      • Both are good at signaling when they need to go outside, so you can rely on their cues as well as the regular potty intervals.
        • Potty Signals:
          • Parker barks near the door, guides you to the door, or sits/lies down by the door.
          • Giles cries or spins for no apparent reason (without anything obvious triggering him, like a loud noise that could scare or stress him out), or gets down from the couch and starts spinning/crying.
    • If you need them inside, you can say "let's go inside" or "come inside," and keep the door wide open. They usually come in then, but not always. Parker especially loves being outside.
    • If they hold a paw up and/or are not moving, they might have a pine needle stuck in their paw. Carefully check their paws and gently pull the needle out.

  • Parker's Water Bowl Check

    • Parker always drinks water right after eating, so his water bowl needs to be checked before feeding.
    • Rinse the bowl with hot tap water and fill it with RO filtered water.
      • If the water bowl is exceptionally dirty, use the dog dish sponge (pink smiley sponge wand) and Dawn dish soap to wash it.

  • Hunger Strikes (Separation Anxiety)

    • Please keep me posted about any hunger strikes.
    • Keep offering water.
    • Try giving a spoonful of cooked ground turkey (located in the fridge) alone in their bowl.

  • Breakfast

    • Serve right after the morning potty break.
    • Empty their pre-portioned food into each of their bowls.
      • Food is packed in Ziploc bags marked for Giles and Parker in the fridge and freezer (please move bags from the freezer to the fridge when you get low on the food in the fridge).
    • Put both bowls together in the microwave.
    • Cover them with small plates.
    • Microwave for 50 seconds.
    • Pour a 1/4 shot glass of RO filtered water into Giles' bowl and a 1/2 shot glass into Parker's.
    • Mix their food with their own corresponding spoons.
      • Separate spoons are offered in case the bowls are not washed every time, since Giles' bowl will have medicine in it.
    • (Breakfast only): Shake Giles' med bottle. Sprinkle 0.5 ml of his medicine into his bowl. Do not mix it in.
    • Rinse the spoons right away for use later.
    • Put Giles' and Parker's bowls on the mats on the floor.
      • Generally, Giles' bowl goes on the mat closer to the kitchen, but you might need to switch the bowls around depending on which mat Giles feels comfortable eating on.
      • If Parker is barking at his bowl:
        • It means: 1) the bowl is wobbly or sliding around when he's trying to eat, or 2) the food is too hot.
      • Helping with Giles' hesitation to eat:
        • Try sitting by Giles and his bowl.
        • Or, settle down away from him.
        • Or, move the mat and bowl closer to him, or rotate the bowl.
        • Or, let Parker out when he finishes his food; Giles might start eating, or he might join Parker outside and then come right back in and start eating while you watch Parker by the door.
        • Or, hand-feed him (only as a last resort).

  • Potty break right after eating

  • Parker's Post-Food Play

    • Parker likes to lick/chew/play with his toy after eating and drinking water.
    • He will most likely want to bring his toy and lie down by you while he does that, or he might want you to engage with him. You can wiggle the toy or do a light game of tug-of-war. Keep the toy and play on his ground level. No standing/jumping for toys, and no fetching.

  • Giles' Water Drinking

    • He usually requests water immediately after waking up and after eating.
    • He will most likely scratch the area around the couch or his surroundings to signal his need to drink water right after eating or whenever he wants water. Or, he might lick his lips and look at the water bottle and you. Or, he might whine and circle on the couch.
    • Put down the large, folded pink towel so you don't soak the couch.
    • Unscrew the clear button inside the bowl counter-clockwise, then squeeze the bottle and hold the squeeze to keep the water in the bowl.
    • Tip the bottle sideways on top of the towels and remain still. Giles will come and lick the side of the bowl to drink water. Expect lots of splashes!
    • As he drinks water, you can squeeze the bottle more to keep the water closer to the brim of the tipped bowl. He struggles to drink if the water level is lower.
    • He will walk away once he's done drinking. If he just pauses from drinking without walking away, you should continue holding the water, as he may resume drinking water.

docs.title: Daytime
docs.text:
• Potty every 3 - 5 hours or when they ask for it.

    • Same instructions as above.
    • Potty Signals:
      • Parker barks near the door, guides you to the door, or sits/lies down by the door.
      • Giles cries or spins for no apparent reason (without an obvious trigger), or gets down the ramp and starts spinning near the door.

  • If Giles hasn't drunk water out of his bottle since waking up, feel free to offer it to him.

  • Play requested by Parker.

  • Quality Chilling/Snuggling/Sleeping Time

    • Giles doesn't play with toys, but he will be happy to come chill near you if he feels comfortable being around you.
    • They both love gentle petting.
      • Giles hates getting touched unexpectedly, with a gripping motion, or on the top of his head and the back of his neck (this is likely due to developing neck arthritis). Always pet him gently directly under his chin and on his chest.

  • Mandatory Snacking Sessions at 11 AM - 12 PM and 3 PM - 4 PM

    • These help reduce bile build-up, especially for Giles. Otherwise, he throws up and refuses food.
    • Duck jerky is broken into small pieces and stored in a Ziploc bag on the kitchen countertop.
    • Give 3 pieces for Giles and 5 pieces for Parker per snacking session.
    • They (especially Parker) love to play "Find It" with the duck jerky pieces. You can hide them at various spots on the floor (behind toys, around the ramp corner, etc.) in the house. With Giles, I recommend you just give the treats directly to him so that you don't have to manage an eager Parker lunging toward him. Also, we want to make sure Giles gets these treats and doesn't have an empty stomach for too long.
    • Giles always asks for treats by scratching his surroundings (sofa, cushions, blankets, and/or the floor) around 11 AM - 12 PM and 3 PM - 4 PM. Even without his cues, please offer the snacks at the suggested times above and see if he takes them.

docs.title: Evening (5 PM - 7 PM)
docs.text:
• Parker's water bowl check

  • Dinner

    • Same "Breakfast" instructions, except Giles does NOT get his medicine this time.
    • If they have been eating reliably, start adding the supplement for dinner only.
      • Put 1 flat scoop (1/2 tsp) of the supplement (the white powder in the Ziploc bag) into Giles' bowl and 2 flat scoops into Parker's.

  • Potty break post-food

  • Parker's post-food play

  • Water for Giles if he requests it

  • Quality chillraxing time

  • Potty every 3 - 5 hours or when they ask for it

  • Maybe a snack session if Giles requests it

docs.title: Bedtime (10 PM - 11 PM)
docs.text:
• Duck jerky snack (3 - 5 pieces per dog) right before bed to prevent bile build-up
  • Final potty before bed
    • Watch out for animals, especially skunks, in the yard before letting them out.
    • Same "Potty" instructions apply.

docs.title: "What do you need/want?"
docs.text:
• Parker understands this phrase and will try to direct/lead you physically or with his gaze. This can be used in general to figure out what he needs (e.g., a certain toy he's asking for or wanting to go outside).
  • Giles may direct you with his gaze in response to your question.

docs.title: "Do you want to go potty/outside?" / "Let's go outside"
docs.text: • They know you are urging them to go outside to go potty.

docs.title: "Where's your toy?"
docs.text: • This is for Parker. He will bring a toy to you to play near or with you.

docs.title: Basic commands
docs.text:
• Sit
  • Down
  • Handshake
  • Other hand
    • Used immediately after a handshake. The dogs will give you their other hand for a handshake.
  • Stay
    • "OK" cancels "stay"
  • Come (here)
